sourceMovie.title: PK
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto PK (2014) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Robot & Frank
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If PK clicked for you, Robot & Frank (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Men in Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If PK clicked for you, Men in Black (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Contact
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If PK clicked for you, Contact (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after PK?
faq.answer: Try Robot & Frank, Men in Black, Contact—each shares DNA with PK while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is PK?
faq.answer: PK (2014) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is PK worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
